flashcards.back: CPU Utilization measures the percentage of time the CPU is actively executing processes. The goal is to maximize it (typically 40-90%).
Throughput is the number of processes completed per unit of time. It indicates the total work done by the CPU.
Turnaround Time is the total time taken from process submission to completion. Lower values improve user experience.
Waiting Time is the time a process spends waiting in the ready queue. The goal is to minimize it to reduce delays.

flashcards.front: Why is Response Time important in interactive systems?
flashcards.back: Response Time is the time from process submission to the first response. It is critical in interactive systems to ensure quick user feedback.
